quiz.title: Unit 3 Quiz 12, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Periodic Trends Review
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Periodic Trends Down Groups, Electron Configuration and Group Identification, Alkali Metals Identification, Nonmetallic Character Ordering, Valence Electrons, Octet Rule, Metal Reactivity

questions.question: One of the following does NOT increase while moving down a group:
questions.explanation: Valence (number of valence electrons) does NOT increase down a group. Elements in the same group have the same number of valence electrons. Atomic radius, number of shells, and shielding effect all increase down a group.

questions.question: An atom has (2, 8, 2) electronic configuration. It belongs to:
questions.explanation: The configuration 2, 8, 2 shows 2 valence electrons in the outermost shell. Elements with 2 valence electrons belong to Group 2 (alkaline earth metals). This atom is magnesium (Mg, Z=12).

questions.question:
Given four elements as shown:
I: Atomic number 9
II: Atomic number 11
III: Atomic number 17
IV: Atomic number 12
V: Atomic number 13.
Which pair belong to the same group?
questions.explanation: Elements with atomic numbers 9 (fluorine) and 17 (chlorine) both belong to Group 17 (halogens). They have the same number of valence electrons (7). Atomic number 11 is sodium (Group 1), 12 is magnesium (Group 2), and 13 is aluminum (Group 13).

questions.question: The valence shell for the elements in period 2 is _____________
questions.explanation: Elements in Period 2 have electrons filling the second energy level, which is the L shell. The valence shell is the outermost occupied shell, so for Period 2 elements, it is the L shell. K shell is Period 1, M shell is Period 3, and N shell is Period 4.

questions.question: Which of the following elements is the poorest conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Sulfur is a nonmetal and a very poor conductor of electricity. Copper and aluminum are excellent conductors (metals). Silicon is a metalloid with semiconducting properties — it conducts better than sulfur but not as well as metals.

questions.question: If an alkali metal is represented by the symbol M, which formula correctly represents its oxide?
questions.explanation: Alkali metals (Group 1) have a +1 oxidation state. Oxygen in oxides has a -2 oxidation state. Therefore, two alkali metal ions are needed to balance one oxide ion, giving the general formula M₂O. MO would imply a +2 oxidation state (alkaline earth metals), MO₂ would imply +4 (some transition metals or nonmetals), and M₂O₃ would imply +3 (Group 13 elements).

questions.question: Which of the following are atomic numbers of Alkali metals?
questions.explanation: Alkali metals (Group 1) have atomic numbers: 3 (Li), 11 (Na), 19 (K), 37 (Rb), 55 (Cs), 87 (Fr). Option A (37, 55, 19, 87) correctly identifies four alkali metals. Option D includes 39 (Yttrium, not alkali metal).

questions.question: In the order of increasing non-metallic character, arrange: Li, O, C, Be, F
questions.explanation: Non-metallic character increases from left to right across a period. In Period 2, the order is Li (metal) → Be (metal) → C (metalloid/nonmetal) → O (nonmetal) → F (nonmetal). The correct increasing order is Li, Be, C, O, F.

questions.question: The largest atomic radius in Group 1 is:
questions.explanation: Francium (Fr) has the largest atomic radius in Group 1. Atomic radius increases down a group as additional electron shells are added. Francium is at the bottom of Group 1, with seven electron shells, giving it the largest atomic radius among alkali metals.

questions.question: Which of the following has the least number of valence electrons?
questions.explanation: Sodium (Na) has 1 valence electron (Group 1). Magnesium has 2, aluminum has 3, and silicon has 4. Therefore, sodium has the least number of valence electrons.

questions.question: The Octet rule applies up to which elements?
questions.explanation: The octet rule applies most consistently to Period 2 and Period 3 elements. These elements (from carbon to chlorine) tend to achieve 8 electrons in their valence shells. Beyond Period 3, elements can expand their octets using d orbitals.

questions.question: Which of the following metals would lose an electron easily?
questions.explanation: Potassium (K) would lose an electron most easily among these options. It has the lowest ionization energy because it is lower in Group 1 than Na, and Group 1 metals lose electrons more easily than Group 2 metals (Mg, Ca). Reactivity increases down Group 1.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered all the periodic trends and group properties! ⚛️📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review valence electron patterns and non-metallic character ordering.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on group identification from electron configuration and alkali metal atomic numbers.
scoreMessages.0: Please review periodic trends, valence electrons, and how to identify elements from their electron configurations.
